meaning: n.小丑,笨人,丑角adj.滑稽的,愚蠢的
examples: Organizers collected a range of bold, sometimes zany, approaches to the threat of an increasingly inhospitable world.
It was a record year with over 35, 000 runners completing the course and lots of world records broken for zany costumes and other fun things.
Some people took the movie at face value: the zany antics of Indian college kids.
Other companies rely on zany programs to prevent boredom while new hires are ramping up.
"It's been a while since an original, zany comedy has been on the marketplace, " says Rocco.
